revision.text:
[[ja:伊達神社 (色麻町)]] {{Expand Japanese|伊達神社 (色麻町)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Idate Shrine | native_name = 伊達神社 | image = Haiden of Idate-jinja shrine.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Idate Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|38.545155|140.851096}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Isotakeru|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|ja|五十猛神|simple|Isotakeru-no-kami|zh|五十猛神|qid=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Idate Shrine'''|伊達神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 21 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039990|ltq=Q135039990|qid=Q135039990|1=Kamiishino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039991|ltq=Q135039991|qid=Q135039991|1=Onsen Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Shikama, Miyagi|lt=Shikama|arz|شيكاما|ceb|Shikama-cho|en|Shikama, Miyagi|es|Shikama (Miyagi)|fa|شیکاما، میاگی|fr|Shikama|id|Shikama, Miyagi|it|Shikama|ja|色麻町|ko|시카마정|ms|Shikama, Miyagi|my|ရှိခမမြို့၊ မိယဂိ|pl|Shikama|ru|Сикама|tl|Shikama, Miyagi|tr|Shikama|uk|Сікама (Міяґі)|uz|Shikama (Miyagi)|zh_yue|色麻町|zh|色麻町|qid=Q1204478|1=Shikama, Miyagi}} * {{ill|Q907389|ltq=Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|38.545155|140.851096}} == image (P18) == * Haiden of Idate-jinja shrine.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q10881556|ltq=Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20151121015627/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/276001.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182058 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|lt=nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|fr|Nagare-zukuri|it|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|pl|Nagare-zukuri|ru|Нагарэ-дзукури|sv|Nagare-zukuri|zh|流造|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 4370205001298 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県加美郡色麻町四竃字町3', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊達神社 (色麻町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11380848}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Idate Shrine |画像 = [[File:Haiden of Idate-jinja shrine.JPG|280px|Haiden]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kami District|Q1155600|qid=Q1155600|1=Kami District, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Shikama|Q1204478|qid=Q1204478|1=Shikama, Miyagi}}Shitate, Aza Machi 3 |位置 ={{Coord|38|32|43.1|N|140|51|04.0|E|region:JP-04_type=landmark|display=inline,title|name=Idate Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isatake-no-Kami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |札所等 = Shikama ancient three shrines |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 28|Q2952|qid=Q2952|1=October 28}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Idate Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Idate Shrine'''（いだてじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shikama|Q1204478|qid=Q1204478|1=Shikama, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Kami District|Q1155600|qid=Q1155600|1=Kami District, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|qid=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It stands on the round tumulus called Oyama Kofun on the left bank of the Hana River, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Naruse River|Q11675397|qid=Q11675397|1=Naruse River}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Main deities * '''{{ill|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isatake-no-Kami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}''' * '''{{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}''' * '''{{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}''' ; Merged deities * {{ill|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|1=Wakahiru-me}} * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Himusubi-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} * Deities of nearby merged shrines: Kumano Shrine, Shiogama Shrine, Tamura Shrine, Ima-Kumano Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, Ogawa Shrine, Tanaka Shrine（{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kunado-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}、Okitama-no-Mikoto、{{ill|lt=Kuni no Tokotachi|Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni no Sokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}、{{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}、{{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|qid=Q11568925|lt=Ietsu-no-Miko-no-Kami, Kumano Fusu-mi-no-Kami, Hayatama-no-o-no-Kami|1=Kumano Gongen}}、{{ill|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime|Q505469|qid=Q505469|lt=Kushiinada-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}}） === History === Tradition says it was enshrined in the Enryaku era (782-806) when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|qid=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} campaigned east. Another tradition says people from around Ikashi District of {{ill|lt=Harima Province|Q1047523|qid=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}} settled here and enshrined Iyoshi Ōkami ({{ill|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru|Q10881556|qid=Q10881556|lt=Isatake-no-Kami|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}) from {{ill|lt=Iho-no-Aramoni Shrine|Q11458547|qid=Q11458547|1=Itatehyōzu Shrine}} in Ikashi, Harima. In the mid-Heian {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it appears as "Mutsu Province, Shikama District, Idate Shrine, myōjin taisha," indicating its listing as {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. When the {{ill|lt=Date clan|Q1151061|qid=Q1151061|1=Date clan}} became governor of Mutsu, the shrine became associated with them, and the name was changed to '''Katori-sha''' in reference to {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|qid=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}}, where one of this shrine's deities, {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, is enshrined. In 1872 (Meiji 5), the name was restored to "Idate Shrine." === Precincts === File:Honden of Idate-jinja shrine.JPG|Honden File:Explanation board of Idate-jinja shrine.JPG|Name plaques
Plaques for former shrine name "Katori-sha" and merged "Kumano Shrine." File:Old Japanese Ginkgo tree of Idate-jinja shrine.JPG|Sacred ginkgo tree File:2nd Torii gate of Idate-jinja shrine.JPG|Second torii File:Main approach to Idate-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach File:Idate-jinja shrine.JPG|Zuishin gate File:1st Torii gate of Idate-jinja shrine.JPG|First torii === Shikama ancient three shrines === {{座標一覧|節=色麻古社三社}} Together with this shrine, the following three are called the "three ancient shrines of Shikama": * Idate Shrine - south of Naruse River * {{ill|lt=Akatsuka Kashima Shrine (Kami)|Q11534936|qid=Q11534936|lt=Akatsuka Kashima Shrine|1=Shibagaki Shrine}}（{{ill|lt=Kami|Q1205414|qid=Q1205414|1=Kami, Miyagi}}Yokkaichiba Aza Yashiki, {{ウィキ座標|38|33|20.6|N|140|51|59.6|E|region:JP|地図|name=Akatsuka Kashima Shrine}}）- north of Naruse River * Ukisu Shrine（Kami-shimo-shinden, {{ウィキ座標|38|32|38.6|N|140|52|31|E|region:JP|地図|name=Ukisu Shrine}}) - north of Naruse River It is said that their triangular placement across the Naruse River was modeled after the "Three Shrines of the East": {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|qid=Q706499|1=Kashima Jingū}} (Ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}), {{ill|lt=Katori Jingū|Q372380|qid=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}} (Ichinomiya of {{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|qid=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}), and {{ill|lt=Ikisu Shrine|Q11073095|qid=Q11073095|1=Ikisu Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kamisu|Q840595|qid=Q840595|1=Kamisu}}, which form a triangle around the {{ill|lt=Tone River|Q177452|qid=Q177452|1=Tone River}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=Greeting|url=http://www.kashimajinja.org/m_aisatu.htm|publisher=Kashima Shrine|accessdate=2012-08-27}} File:Haiden of Akatsuka Kashima-jinja shrine in Kami town..JPG|Akatsuka Kashima Shrine File:Haiden of Ukisu-jinja shrine in Kami town..JPG|Ukisu Shrine === References === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020407 Idate Shrine]（Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Office） * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/276001.html Idate Shrine]（Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE "Compilation of Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials"） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Idate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Idate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Idate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:色麻町の建築物]] [[Category:色麻町の歴史]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]]
